author: @charlesdickens6706
text: .....Rob , if you like I can send you some small unfilled lead acid batteries, transparent so can view stuff happening inside when evaluating various claims for various substances to add as described in some u toob videos. 425 grams each, 6 volts each. I've recently tested a few things out myself including the sodium silicate. Next I'll test addition of cadmium sulphate to see about the claims made for it as it was once reputedly marketed to hold back sulphate formation and more. It's excellent when we can view the inside of the cells for assessment of desulphation methods or whatever else takes fancy.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: wow - that's a very generous offer mate - I'd certainly be up for that - drop me a line at robertmurraysmith64@gmail.com - cheers

author: @jayb3843
text: I know you're working on other projects, but if I become a member will you teach me how to make the graphene enhanced plastic? Thanks for all your videos! You do amazing work I don't mind supporting regardless i just am  really extremely interested in that topic specifically.  Thanks !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I might try to do that but I know the number of things I have to do and I would hate to say yes and then let you down by just not having the time to do it - so no - I can't promise to do that - sorry mate - glad you like the channel - cheers

replies.author: @jayb3843
replies.text: thank you for the promt reply! I feel terrible I am just now seeing this! I have been steadily watching all of your videos sense the time I made this comment and do not regret a second of it! I was still very new at this time and have learned alot! From what I have gathered the issue would be creating the graphene more so than mixing with a polymer, I know there are many methods of creating graphene including "flash" graphene but from what I understand this is not proper graphene like that we have been hearing about from science for the past few years, while it does appear to be simillar if you look under a microscope the carbon does not seem to form the individual shape that interlocks and is required as that is what give the graphene it's desired property's. I would love to see more graphene video's tho! Thanks again and keep up the good work.

author: @noelwellstead8744

author: @bretthorwood9396
text: Dear Robert thanks to your interesting channel. I just thought id make a zinc bromime cell and see how it goes. Firstly i purchased Na Br  and zn sulphate off ebay.i made the zinc bromide from this. And just making the cell now using a container for fish oil capsules about a 1L capacity we will see if i made the electrolyte right.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is awesome mate

author: @tkmotors991
text: The thing is is space savers are very heavy probably not practical for electric vehicles of this size

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they are lighter than the tyres I took off

replies.author: @tkmotors991
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering that’s pretty hard to believe because space savers are solid rubber with heavier steel they didn’t call them weight savers

replies.author: @tkmotors991
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering not trying to bag you or anything great work

author: @SeraphimCherubim
text: Have you thought about making your own Vanadium flow batteries? I think you are probably smart enough to figure out a DIY version.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes mate there is just no point to them below 50kW - the support cost of the pumping and pipe work makes them more expensive for storage below 50kW when you might as well use static batteries as they are cheaper overall to implement a system

author: @DragonZarman
text: Hi Rob! My partner and I are designing a prototype based on your solid-state wind generator. It is the one that uses a pvc pipe with an artificial muscle attached to it. We were wondering how you obtained the coil you used, since when we tried building our own, we were not able to get any current through the vibrations.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I made the muscle in line with a research paper where they were made from nylon fishing line here is the link https://www.researchgate.net/publication/260372180_Artificial_Muscles_from_Fishing_Line_and_Sewing_Thread

author: @chuxxsss
text: Good morning Rob, help a guy out yesterday night, put apart some laptop displays. He had a speedo meter near the windscreen as his car does not give an accurate measurement of speed. His reflector needed replacing.  I said I have just the thing. Parts of the display sheets  work as a reflector for me. Give them to him. He was here for speaker cable for a caravan he was building. Never throw out what others can use, is my theory now.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - awesome stuff mate and I agree 100 with your theory lol

author: @safffff1000
text: This is interesting but when are we going to see the wind turbines running for some days on the roof or see the gasifier producing gas and running  some motor or such?

replies.author: @sweltermayo
replies.text: We only get out wotwe put in

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I did both mate - but it's just not interesting to most people - a few will watch for sure but most don't - so really there is no point from my perspective to videos like that - on another note loads of people have done gasifiers running engines so if it works we know it will do that - equally with wind turbines if the produce stick 'em in the wind and you will see them generate - we know they do that - so I only went as far as proof of concept the rest like SteHeych says is really up to you - if you like it build it and see - for myself there is only so far I can go with individual projects and when they are done - well basically they are done - sorry mate

author: @William_Hada
text: Looking good Rob! I can't wait ago see you tooling around on it!
